content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 6, 2005

Heather K. Whitham
City of Torrance
Office of the City Attorney
3031 Torrance Blvd.
Torrance, CA 90503-5059

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-215

Dear Ms. Witham:

This letter is in response to your request for reconsideration dated October 19, 2005. As stated in our initial response to your inquiry (W-05-163), because your inquiry involves past conduct, we decline to provide you with written advice. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

Though the advice you seek may be at least partially relevant to future contemplated acts of the Torrance Airport Commissioners, such prospective acts are inextricably tied to past conduct (i.e., recommendations the commissioners have already forwarded to the city council).

As you indicated in your original correspondence, the City of Torrance operates a public airport on land owned by the city. In connection with its airport operations, the city leases land to a number of fixed base operators, private enterprises that provide such aeronautical services as aircraft maintenance, fueling, flight training and hangar and aircraft tie-downs. The city itself also directly rents hangars and tie-downs.

City staff presented a proposed revision to the city's hangar rental agreement for the Torrance Airport Commission's review and comments. The commission in turn delegated the initial review of the proposed hangar rental agreement to an ad hoc committee for its consideration.

The city council invites applications for commission appointment from Torrance residents. Frequently the council appoints members of the public who have a particular interest in the subject matter jurisdiction of a commission. As a result three current

File No. W-05-215
Page No. 2

Airport Commissioners own planes and rent hangars from the city. A fourth
Commissioner owns an equity interest in a flying club based at Torrance Airport; he
apparently owns an undivided partial ownership interest in the club's aircraft, which are
based at Torrance Airport. The club also rents hangar space at the airport form the city.

Both the ad hoc committee and the airport commission have considered the
proposed revisions to the city's hangar rental agreement. Proposed revisions include
adding an automatic cost-of-living rent escalator, establishing different hangar rental
rates for Torrance residents and non-residents, heightened insurance requirements and
broader indemnity requirements. The proposed revisions will have an effect not only on
hangar rental rates but also on other costs of basing aircraft at the Torrance Airport.

The Airport Commission's comments and recommendations regarding the
proposed revision to the city's hangar rental agreement had already been forwarded to the
city council for its consideration at the time of your original correspondence. In
reviewing matters brought before it, the city council regularly, although not always,
accepts the recommendations of the city's commissions.

Because your question is about the obligation of the Airport Commissioners, and
because they had already made recommendations and forwarded them to the city council
prior to your seeking advice on the Commissioners' behalf, we must decline your request.

We again reiterate that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed
in your request, and that nothing in the Political Reform Act prevents an official from
seeking legal advice from you or private counsel.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Andreas C. Rockas
Counsel, Legal Division

ACR:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\05-215

sections.conclusion: Because your question is about the obligation of the Airport Commissioners, and because they had already made recommendations and forwarded them to the city council prior to your seeking advice on the Commissioners' behalf, we must decline your request.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Airport Commissioners who own planes, rent hangars, or have equity interests in flying clubs have conflicts of interest in reviewing and recommending changes to the city's hangar rental agreement that would affect their own rental costs.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the inquiry involves past conduct (recommendations already forwarded to city council) rather than prospective acts. The advice request cannot be separated from the past conduct already completed by the Commissioners.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter citing procedural basis (past conduct). No formal QUESTION section header present but legal issue is clear from context. Document references prior advice letter W-05-163 and request for reconsideration dated October 19, 2005.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Airport Commissioners who own planes, rent hangars, or have equity interests in flying clubs have conflicts of interest in reviewing and recommending changes to the city's hangar rental agreement that would affect their own rental costs.

CONCLUSION: Because your question is about the obligation of the Airport Commissioners, and because they had already made recommendations and forwarded them to the city council prior to your seeking advice on the Commissioners' behalf, we must decline your request.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice to the City of Torrance regarding potential conflicts of interest for Airport Commissioners in reviewing hangar rental agreement revisions, citing Regulation 1832e(b)(8)(A) because the inquiry involves past conduct and recommendations already forwarded to the city council.
